name: idea-refine
description_raw: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".
description_normalized: Turns vague ideas into sharp, actionable concepts via structured divergent (expand) then convergent (stress-test, converge) thinking.

frontmatter_yaml.name: idea-refine
frontmatter_yaml.description: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".

name: interview-me
description_raw: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified, when the user explicitly invokes, or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.
description_normalized: Closes the gap between what users ask for and what they actually want via one-question-at-a-time interview before any plan or code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: interview-me
frontmatter_yaml.description: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified ("build me X" without "for whom" or "why now"), when the user explicitly invokes ("interview me", "grill me", "are we sure?", "stress-test my thinking"), or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.

name: planning-and-task-breakdown
description_raw: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.
description_normalized: Decomposes specs into small, verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and explicit dependency ordering.

frontmatter_yaml.name: planning-and-task-breakdown
frontmatter_yaml.description: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.

name: plan
description_raw: Break work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes planning-and-task-breakdown to decompose a spec into verifiable, ordered implementation tasks.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Break work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering

name: architectural-review
description_raw: Structural and design quality review of system architecture. Different from code review — this asks "is this system well-structured for growth?" not "is this code correct?". Use when user says "architectural review", "system design review", "is this ready to scale", "structural review", "before adding a major feature", "God class", "too many responsibilities", "design review", "is this maintainable", "refactor planning", "before building on top", "architecture audit", "is this well designed", "responsibility review". Does NOT replace code-quality-audit or test-quality-audit — it adds a third lens on top of them.
description_normalized: Structural and design quality review of system architecture.

name: bmad-story-lifecycle
description_raw: Launches and manages the complete BMAD story lifecycle using Claude Code multi-agent teammate orchestration. Use when user wants to "run a BMAD story", "start the story lifecycle", "run story 6.6", "bmad story run", "orchestrate a story", "run the full BMAD pipeline", "start from Amelia", "skip Bob", "bmad orchestrate". Produces infrastructure start block, orchestrator system prompt, and paste-ready agent command sequence for a given story ID.
description_normalized: Launches and manages the complete BMAD story lifecycle using Claude Code multi-agent teammate orchestration.

name: goal-plan
description_raw: Interview the user through a structured plan schema (Stack, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Acceptance Criteria, Key References) and produce two artifacts — a plan markdown document and a paste-ready 1-line /goal condition for Claude Code's autonomous goal feature (v2.1.139+, max 4000 chars). Use when the user says "goal plan", "build a plan", "set a goal", "spec this", "plan for goal", "plan for /goal", "make me a plan", "plan this out before we build", "I want to use /goal", or any variation of "build a structured plan before invoking /goal". Supports four interview modes — code, content, research, freeform — selected up front so the questions match the work. Does NOT execute the plan; produces the inputs that /goal then runs.
description_normalized: Interview the user through a structured plan schema (Stack, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Acceptance Criteria, Key References) and produce two artifacts — a plan markdown document and a paste-ready 1-line /goal condition for Claude Code's autonomous goal feature (v2.1.139+, max 4000 chars).

name: omi-extract-haiku
description_raw: Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the Claude Code harness (Max plan credits — no external API, no Gemini quota). Spawns parallel Haiku background agents for isolated per-batch classification. Adds extraction_model: haiku to distinguish from Gemini-extracted files. Use when: 'extract with haiku', 'haiku extract', 'omi haiku', 'intake extract haiku', 'compare extraction models', 'test haiku on omi files', 'run haiku extractor'.
description_normalized: Extract and classify OMI conversation transcripts using Haiku model via the Claude Code harness (Max plan credits — no external API, no Gemini quota).

name: omi-query
description_raw: Query OMI wearable transcripts by brain, routing type, activity, or recency. Use when user says: "what did I record about X", "search omi for X", "omi backlog", "brain-update backlog", "what's pending brain updates", "what did omi capture about X", "recent omi sessions", "omi this week", "omi sessions mentioning X", "show me omi todo items", "what did I learn this week", "omi til entries", "omi planning sessions". Reads frontmatter tags written by omi-extract. Requires omi-extract to have run first.
description_normalized: Query OMI wearable transcripts by brain, routing type, activity, or recency.

name: ralphy
description_raw: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects. Use when user says "ralphy", "run ralphy", "start a ralph campaign", "new campaign", "new PR", "new worktree", "start a wave", "plan this", "build this", "extend this", "add features", "run a coordinator loop", "what does ralphy do", "switch to analysis", "switch to development", "switch to content", "switch profile", or any mention of running an autonomous batch of work (planning, building, extending) on an existing project. Ralphy has four modes: Requirements, Plan, Build, Extend — and three campaign profiles: Development, Analysis, Content. He is directive — he reads current state and tells the user where things are and what's available.
description_normalized: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects.

name: relay-register
description_raw: Manage relay folders — typed shared-folder channels between agent harnesses and apps, or between people. Use when user mentions: \"list relay folders\", \"relay status\", \"relay info\", \"register a relay\", \"create a relay folder\", \"open syncthing\", \"app relay for angeleye\", \"app relay with paperclip\", \"app relay with hermes\", \"people relay for Jan\", \"people relay for Lars\", \"external relay\", \"relay for david-lars\", \"project relay\", \"flihub relay\", \"migrate relay\", \"relay handover\", \"register relay from handover\", \"set up sync folder\", \"relay for beauty-and-joy\", \"relay for supportsignal\", \"what would a relay folder look like\".
description_normalized: Manage relay folders — typed shared-folder channels between agent harnesses and apps, or between people.

name: session-checkpoint
description_raw: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly. Use when user says "checkpoint", "save my progress", "checkpoint the workflow", "where am I up to", "save task state", "checkpoint before this next step", or when about to do a risky operation or context is filling up. Captures step-by-step progress through a plan or task list — NOT a session summary (use capture-context) and NOT durable knowledge (use knowledge-capture).
description_normalized: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly.

name: po-templates
description_raw: Scaffold product documentation files for FliVideo projects. Use when (1) starting a new project that needs docs structure, (2) PO needs to create backlog.md, changelog.md, or brainstorming-notes.md, (3) creating a new PRD file for a requirement. Creates files in docs/ relative to current project.
description_normalized: Scaffold product documentation files for FliVideo projects.

Use when the user says "let's brainstorm", "what should we build", or "help me think through X", presents a vague or ambitious feature request, or seems unsure about scope or direction -- even without explicitly asking to brainstorm.

name: ce-plan
description_raw: Create structured plans for multi-step tasks -- software features, research workflows, events, study plans, or any goal that benefits from breakdown. Also deepens existing plans with interactive sub-agent review. Use when the user says 'plan this', 'create a plan', 'how should we build', 'break this down', or when a brainstorm doc is ready for planning.
description_normalized: Creates structured implementation plans from any input (requirements doc, bug report, feature idea) and optionally deepens them via interactive subagent review.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Create structured plans for multi-step tasks.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [optional: feature description, requirements doc path, plan path to deepen, or any task to plan]

description_raw: Create or maintain STRATEGY.md - the product's target problem, approach, users, key metrics, and tracks of work. Use when starting a new product, updating direction, or when prompts like 'write our strategy', 'update the roadmap', 'what are we working on', or 'set up the strategy doc' come up.
description_normalized: Creates and maintains STRATEGY.md as a short durable anchor document capturing product target, approach, users, metrics, and investment tracks.

name: blueprint
description_raw: Turn a one-line objective into a step-by-step construction plan for multi-session, multi-agent engineering projects. Each step has a self-contained context brief so a fresh agent can execute it cold. Includes adversarial review gate, dependency graph, parallel step detection, anti-pattern catalog, and plan mutation protocol. TRIGGER when: user requests a plan, blueprint, or roadmap for a complex multi-PR task, or describes work that needs multiple sessions. DO NOT TRIGGER when: task is completable in a single PR or fewer than 3 tool calls, or user says "just do it".
description_normalized: Turn a one-line objective into a step-by-step construction plan for multi-session, multi-agent engineering projects.

name: production-scheduling

Informed by production schedulers with 15+ years experience. Includes TOC/drum-buffer-rope, SMED, OEE analysis, disruption response frameworks, and ERP/MES interaction patterns. Use when scheduling production, resolving bottlenecks, optimizing changeovers, responding to disruptions, or balancing manufacturing lines.

name: gsd:add-backlog
description_raw: Add an idea to the backlog parking lot (999.x numbering). Add a backlog item to the roadmap using 999.x numbering. Backlog items are unsequenced ideas that aren't ready for active planning — they live outside the normal phase sequence and accumulate context over time.
description_normalized: Adds an unsequenced idea to the roadmap backlog using 999.x phase numbering.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-backlog
frontmatter_yaml.description: Add an idea to the backlog parking lot (999.x numbering)

description_raw: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap. Handles: phase number calculation (next sequential integer), directory creation with slug generation, roadmap structure updates, STATE.md roadmap evolution tracking.
description_normalized: Appends a new sequential integer phase to the end of the current milestone roadmap.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap

name: gsd:add-todo
description_raw: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context. Handles: directory structure creation, content extraction from arguments or conversation, area inference from file paths, duplicate detection and resolution, todo file creation with frontmatter.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-todo
frontmatter_yaml.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context

description_raw: Analyze phase dependencies and suggest Depends on entries for ROADMAP.md. Analyzes the phase dependency graph for the current milestone: file overlap, semantic dependencies, data flow.
description_normalized: Analyzes the phase dependency graph and suggests ROADMAP.md Depends-on entries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:analyze-dependencies
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyze phase dependencies and suggest Depends on entries for ROADMAP.md

name: gsd:check-todos
description_raw: List pending todos and select one to work on. Handles: todo counting and listing with area filtering, interactive selection with full context loading, roadmap correlation checking, action routing (work now, add to phase, brainstorm, create phase), STATE.md updates.
description_normalized: Lists pending todos, allows selection, and routes to the appropriate action (work now, promote to phase, etc.).

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:check-todos
frontmatter_yaml.description: List pending todos and select one to work on

description_normalized: Gathers phase implementation decisions through adaptive questioning, producing CONTEXT.md consumed by researcher and planner.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:discuss-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning

description_normalized: Inserts a decimal-numbered urgent phase between existing integer phases without renumbering the roadmap.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:insert-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:new-project
frontmatter_yaml.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md

description_normalized: Creates ROADMAP phases to close all gaps identified by /gsd-audit-milestone.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit

name: gsd:plan-phase
description_raw: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop. Orchestrates: 4 parallel phase-researcher agents → research synthesizer → planner → plan-checker (loop ≤3). Supports --auto, --research, --skip-research, --gaps, --skip-verify, --prd, --reviews, --text flags.
description_normalized: Orchestrates parallel research, planning, and verification to produce a PLAN.md for a phase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:plan-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop

description_normalized: Captures a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions that surfaces automatically at the right milestone.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:plant-seed
frontmatter_yaml.description: Capture a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions — surfaces automatically at the right milestone

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:remove-phase

frontmatter_yaml.description: Review and promote backlog items to active milestone

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-assumptions-analyzer

description_normalized: Researches phase implementation and produces RESEARCH.md answering 'what do I need to know to plan this phase well?'

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-phase-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator.

description_normalized: Verifies that plans will actually achieve the phase goal via goal-backward analysis before execution.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-plan-checker
frontmatter_yaml.description: Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-planner

name: autoplan
description_raw:
Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk
and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles. Surfaces
taste decisions (close approaches, borderline scope, codex disagreements) at a final
approval gate. One command, fully reviewed plan out.
Use when asked to "auto review", "autoplan", "run all reviews", "review this plan
automatically", or "make the decisions for me".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan file and wants to run the full review
gauntlet without answering 15-30 intermediate questions. (gstack)
description_normalized: Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk

name: design-consultation
description_raw:
Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a
complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and
generates font+color preview pages. Creates DESIGN.md as your project's design source
of truth. For existing sites, use /plan-design-review to infer the system instead.
Use when asked to "design system", "brand guidelines", or "create DESIGN.md".
Proactively suggest when starting a new project's UI with no existing
design system or DESIGN.md. (gstack)
description_normalized: Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a

name: office-hours
description_raw:
YC Office Hours — two modes. Startup mode: six forcing questions that expose
demand reality, status quo, desperate specificity, narrowest wedge, observation,
and future-fit. Builder mode: design thinking brainstorming for side projects,
hackathons, learning, and open source. Saves a design doc.
Use when asked to "brainstorm this", "I have an idea", "help me think through
this", "office hours", or "is this worth building".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT answer directly) when the user describes
a new product idea, asks whether something is worth building, wants to think
through design decisions for something that doesn't exist yet, or is exploring
a concept before any code is written.
Use before /plan-ceo-review or /plan-eng-review. (gstack)
description_normalized: YC Office Hours — two modes. Startup mode: six forcing questions that expose

name: plan-ceo-review
description_raw:
CEO/founder-mode plan review. Rethink the problem, find the 10-star product,
challenge premises, expand scope when it creates a better product. Four modes:
SCOPE EXPANSION (dream big), SELECTIVE EXPANSION (hold scope + cherry-pick
expansions), HOLD SCOPE (maximum rigor), SCOPE REDUCTION (strip to essentials).
Use when asked to "think bigger", "expand scope", "strategy review", "rethink this",
or "is this ambitious enough".
Proactively suggest when the user is questioning scope or ambition of a plan,
or when the plan feels like it could be thinking bigger. (gstack)
description_normalized: CEO/founder-mode plan review. Rethink the problem, find the 10-star product,

name: plan-design-review
description_raw:
Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.
Rates each design dimension 0-10, explains what would make it a 10,
then fixes the plan to get there. Works in plan mode. For live site
visual audits, use /design-review. Use when asked to "review the design plan"
or "design critique".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan with UI/UX components that
should be reviewed before implementation. (gstack)
description_normalized: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.

name: plan-devex-review
description_raw:
Interactive developer experience plan review. Explores developer personas,
benchmarks against competitors, designs magical moments, and traces friction
points before scoring. Three modes: DX EXPANSION (competitive advantage),
DX POLISH (bulletproof every touchpoint), DX TRIAGE (critical gaps only).
Use when asked to "DX review", "developer experience audit", "devex review",
or "API design review".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan for developer-facing products
(APIs, CLIs, SDKs, libraries, platforms, docs). (gstack)
description_normalized: Interactive developer experience plan review. Explores developer personas,

name: plan-eng-review
description_raw:
Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,
data flow, diagrams, edge cases, test coverage, performance. Walks through
issues interactively with opinionated recommendations. Use when asked to
"review the architecture", "engineering review", or "lock in the plan".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan or design doc and is about to
start coding — to catch architecture issues before implementation. (gstack)
description_normalized: Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,

name: brainstorming
description_raw: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation. Guides a structured spec-and-design dialogue: one question at a time, 2-3 approach proposals, design approval gate, write design doc, spec self-review, then hand off to writing-plans.
description_normalized: Structured design dialogue skill that turns ideas into approved spec documents before any implementation begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: brainstorming
frontmatter_yaml.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

name: writing-plans
description_raw: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code. Writes comprehensive implementation plans: file structure map first, then bite-sized tasks (2-5 min steps) with exact file paths, complete code, exact commands and expected output. No placeholders. Every plan includes required REQUIRED SUB-SKILL header. Ends with execution handoff choice (subagent-driven vs inline).
description_normalized: Implementation plan authoring skill that produces zero-placeholder, TDD-structured, bite-sized task plans with complete code at each step.

frontmatter_yaml.name: writing-plans
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
